item.title: [UPDATED] Russian APT Gamaredon Enhances Malware and Evasion Techniques in Ukraine War
item.description:
The Russian APT group Gamaredon has continued to evolve its malware arsenal and evasion tactics in its relentless cyber campaign against Ukraine. Recent analysis shows the group is increasingly using legitimate cloud services like Cloudflare for C2, collaborating with other Russian APTs like Turla, ...

Severity: High

Categories: Threat Actor, Cyberattack, Malware

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] US Government Orders Anthropic to Halt Foreign Access to Advanced AI Models
item.description:
The U.S. government has directed AI firm Anthropic to suspend access to its latest advanced AI models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, for all foreign nationals, citing national security concerns. The move, which occurred on June 13, 2026, marks the most significant U.S. government intervention to date in res...

Severity: High

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] CISA Faces Critical Staffing Shortages Amidst Escalating Cyber Threats
item.description:
More than a year after significant workforce reductions mandated by the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) is reportedly struggling with critical staffing shortages. Lawmakers are voicing serious concerns that the loss of roug...

Severity: High

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Security Operations

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] ShinyHunters Exploits Oracle PeopleSoft Zero-Day to Breach Insurance Regulators
item.description:
The U.S. National Association of Insurance Commissioners (NAIC) has confirmed it was breached by the ShinyHunters threat group, who exploited a critical zero-day vulnerability in Oracle PeopleSoft (CVE-2026-35273). The flaw, rated 9.8 on the CVSS scale, allows for unauthenticated remote code executi...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Data Breach, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] Attackers Actively Exploit Critical Cisco Unified CM Flaw to Deploy Webshells
item.description:
A critical Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerability in Cisco Unified Communications Manager (Unified CM), tracked as CVE-2026-20230, is being actively exploited in the wild. Attackers are leveraging the flaw, which carries an 8.6 CVSS score, to write arbitrary files to the underlying OS, inc...

Severity: High

Categories: Vulnerability, Cyberattack, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] Ransomware Attacks in Europe Skyrocket by 55% as Supply Chains Become Prime Targets
item.description:
A new report from cyber risk management firm Black Kite reveals a staggering 55.1% year-over-year increase in ransomware attacks across Europe in the first four months of 2026. The manufacturing sector was the most heavily targeted industry. Five countries—Germany, the UK, France, Italy, and Spain—a...

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Threat Intelligence, Supply Chain Attack

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] Tata Electronics Confirms Cyberattack; Hackers Claim Leak of Apple and Tesla Data
item.description:
Indian manufacturing giant Tata Electronics, a key supplier for Apple, has confirmed it sustained a cyberattack. The admission follows claims by a hacking group called 'World Leaks' that it stole and published over 630GB of sensitive data. The leaked files allegedly include intellectual property, te...

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Supply Chain Attack, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] Accenture Bets $4.175B on OT Security, Acquiring Dragos, runZero, and NetRise
item.description:
Accenture is making a monumental $4.175 billion investment to secure a leading position in the operational technology (OT) security market. The consulting giant is acquiring a majority stake in OT threat intelligence leader Dragos, while fully acquiring asset discovery firm runZero and firmware secu...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Industrial Control Systems, Policy and Compliance, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] CISA Adds Actively Exploited PTC and Cisco Flaws to KEV Catalog, Mandates Federal Patching
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has added two new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, confirming they are under active exploitation. The flaws are CVE-2026-12569, an improper input validation bug in PTC's Windchill and FlexPLM produc...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management, Regulatory

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] Linux Foundation Launches 'Akrites' Framework to Secure Open Source from AI Threats
item.description:
The Linux Foundation, along with major tech companies like Google, Microsoft, and OpenAI, has launched 'Akrites,' a new cross-industry security framework. The initiative aims to create a unified process for vulnerability disclosure and remediation in critical open-source software. This move is a dir...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Security Operations, Supply Chain Attack

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] OpenAI's 'Daybreak' AI Discovers 24 New Linux Exploits in Mass Scan
item.description:
OpenAI has revealed 'Project Daybreak,' in which its next-generation AI model, GPT-5.5-Cyber, autonomously discovered 24 new Linux privilege escalation exploits by analyzing 30 million lines of code. The AI also found a 29-year-old vulnerability in the widely used Squid web proxy. The achievement si...

Severity: High

Categories: Threat Intelligence, Vulnerability, Malware

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] German Intelligence (BND) Warns Businesses of Russian Cyber Threats After Official Targeted in Phishing Campaign
item.description:
Germany's foreign intelligence service, the Bundesnachrichtendienst (BND), is issuing direct warnings to the nation's business leaders about the heightened risk of Russian cyberattacks. The alert comes after the revelation that a senior BND official was among the victims of a recent, sophisticated R...

Severity: High

Categories: Threat Intelligence, Phishing, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] New 'Icarus' Extortion Group Hits Klue, Steals Customer Salesforce Data via OAuth Attack
item.description:
The competitive intelligence platform Klue has confirmed a security breach where attackers gained unauthorized access to its customers' connected Salesforce environments. A new extortion group named 'Icarus' has claimed responsibility. The attack vector involved the theft and abuse of OAuth tokens, ...

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Threat Actor, Cloud Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] Five Eyes Alliance: AI Reshaping Cyber Threat Landscape in Months, Not Years
item.description:
The 'Five Eyes' intelligence alliance, comprising Australia, Canada, New Zealand, the UK, and the US, has issued a joint statement warning that frontier AI models are dramatically accelerating the speed, scale, and sophistication of cyberattacks. The agencies warn that the timeline for AI-driven thr...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Threat Intelligence, Regulatory

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] The AI Sword: Anthropic Model Demonstrates Hacking Prowess Surpassing Human Experts
item.description:
A new AI model from Anthropic, named Claude Mythos Preview, has demonstrated the ability to autonomously find and exploit software vulnerabilities at a scale and speed that surpasses most human security experts. The model has already been used to find thousands of high-severity flaws in major operat...

Severity: High

Categories: Threat Intelligence, Other

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] 'Atomic Arch' Supply Chain Attack Hijacks Orphaned Linux Packages to Target Developers
item.description:
A sophisticated supply chain attack dubbed 'Atomic Arch' is targeting Linux developers by hijacking trusted but orphaned packages in the Arch User Repository (AUR). Attackers claim ownership of these abandoned packages and modify their build scripts (PKGBUILD) to fetch and install a malicious npm pa...

Severity: High

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Malware, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] Klue Supply Chain Breach Exposes Customer Salesforce Data via Compromised OAuth Tokens
item.description:
The market intelligence platform Klue has suffered a significant supply chain breach after a compromised legacy credential allowed an attacker to harvest OAuth tokens. The 'Icarus' extortion group has claimed responsibility, stating they used the stolen tokens to access and exfiltrate sensitive data...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Data Breach, Cloud Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] AI Accelerating Ransomware, Outpacing Traditional Defenses, Experts Warn
item.description:
At the Infosecurity Europe 2026 conference, experts warned that artificial intelligence (AI) is fundamentally reshaping the cybercrime economy, particularly ransomware. A former high-ranking FBI official stated that AI is making attacks more sophisticated for advanced actors and more accessible for ...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Threat Intelligence, Ransomware, Policy and Compliance

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] Cybersecurity Leaders Condemn US Ban on Anthropic's Advanced AI Models
item.description:
The U.S. government has ordered Anthropic to halt access to its most powerful AI models, Mythos 5 and Fable 5, for all foreign nationals, citing national security risks after a third party reportedly 'jailbroke' the system's security guardrails. In response, Anthropic disabled the models for all use...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: [UPDATED] New 'Agentjacking' Attack Turns AI Coding Assistants into Malicious Insiders
item.description:
Researchers have detailed a novel attack vector called 'Agentjacking,' which allows threat actors to hijack AI coding assistants and execute arbitrary code on a developer's machine. The attack exploits the trust AI agents place in diagnostic platforms like Sentry. By finding a public Sentry DSN, an ...

Severity: High

Categories: Cyberattack, Cloud Security, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →
